decks.notes: This UWx Control deck focuses on using counterspells and removal to control the game, while also developing card advantage through cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Thundertrap Trainer provides a win condition that can quickly take over the game if left unchecked. The deck also has some prison elements with Magus of the Moon and Dress Down to disrupt opponents' mana bases or abilities. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue opponents through card advantage and disruption, ultimately leading to a victory through attrition.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of cards such as Dark Ritual, Doomsday, and Thassa's Oracle to assemble a winning combo that can quickly secure victory. The deck focuses on drawing, filtering, and protection spells to set up the Doomsday stack and win the game with minimal interference from the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Force of Will, Daze, and Thoughtseize help to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan to ensure a successful execution of the Doomsday combo.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, and Amped Raptor provide strong creatures that can quickly deal damage. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Static Prison slow down the opponent's plans, giving the deck an edge in the early game. The inclusion of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Goblin Bombardment helps to control the board and clear the way for attacking. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure and end the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on assembling the pieces needed to cast Doomsday and win the game through a combo with Thassa's Oracle. The deck includes a variety of cantrips and fast mana cards to quickly find and cast Doomsday, while also including disruption spells like Force of Will and Daze to protect the combo. The inclusion of Lion's Eye Diamond allows for additional mana acceleration in the combo turn. The deck also includes ways to filter through the deck with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to set up the perfect Doomsday pile for the win.

The main idea of this deck is to ramp up mana quickly using Cloudpost and Tron lands, then use powerful Eldrazi creatures and artifacts like Karn's Sylex and The One Ring to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also utilizes disruption cards like Warping Wail and Crop Rotation to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win condition. Additionally, Karn, the Great Creator can search for key artifacts to further enhance the control aspect of the deck. Overall, the deck focuses on ramping up mana, controlling the game, and finishing off with powerful Eldrazi threats.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures and using powerful anthem effects like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory to pump up your team. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts your opponent's spells, while cards like Swords to Plowshares and Static Prison help control the board. With a mix of efficient creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with early pressure and disrupt their game plan to secure a fast victory. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands for consistency.

The main focus of this deck is to use Mystic Forge in combination with artifacts like Grim Monolith and Manifold Key to generate a lot of mana and cast powerful spells quickly. Cards like Chalice of the Void and Urza's Saga help control the board and find key pieces of the combo. Glaring Fleshraker can be sacrificed to keep the opponent's board clear while The Fantasticar and The One Ring provide additional support and utility. The deck aims to win by overwhelming the opponent with a quick and efficient combo strategy.

The deck focuses on cheating out powerful creatures such as Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty early in the game using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The deck also includes ways to protect these creatures with counterspells like Force of Will and disruption with cards like Flusterstorm. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with these high-impact creatures and win the game quickly before the opponent can respond effectively. The deck relies on fast mana acceleration with cards like Lotus Petal and lands like Ancient Tomb to set up these powerful combos.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, backed up by powerful spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also utilizes enchantments like Static Prison to control the board and generate value from sacrificing creatures with Goblin Bombardment. The mana base is streamlined with fetch lands and utility lands like Karakas, allowing for consistent and explosive plays. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells before they can stabilize.

The deck focuses on generating mana through creatures like Birds of Paradise and Gaea's Cradle to ramp up quickly and play powerful threats like Craterhoof Behemoth and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. It also includes various control elements such as Endurance and Collector Ouphe to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The main strategy is to efficiently ramp into threats while also having answers to disrupt the opponent's strategy, ultimately leading to a powerful board presence and control of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to generate a large amount of mana quickly by combining various elf creatures and Gaea's Cradle, allowing the player to cast powerful spells such as Natural Order and Green Sun's Zenith to search for key combo pieces like Craterhoof Behemoth. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a strong board presence and win the game in a single explosive turn. Additionally, the deck includes disruption elements like Allosaurus Shepherd to protect the combo and ensure its success.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Once Upon a Time to enable the powerful combo of Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis. Using cards like Elvish Reclaimer and Wight of the Reliquary to support the graveyard strategy, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a strong board presence and efficient removal like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize. The land base includes cards like Bojuka Bog and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's strategies, while also utilizing lands like Boseiju, Who Endures for protection or Eumidian Hatchery for additional creatures.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing lands effectively. It includes cards that disrupt opponents' strategies, slow down their plays, and generate value through land recursion and land-based interactions. The deck's win condition revolves around assembling powerful land combinations such as Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage or utilizing Urza's Saga to create strong token-generating lands. With a mix of disruption, value generation, and powerful land interactions, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and grind out victories through careful resource management and strategic land plays.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently get out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier by utilizing the card Show and Tell to cheat them onto the battlefield. This combo relies on using cards like Lotus Petal, Brainstorm, and Ponder to set up the perfect hand, while also including protection with Force of Will and Spell Pierce. Utilizing cards like Omniscience and Unite the Coalition allows for extra synergy and ramp in order to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and spells. The deck also includes various lands to ensure consistent mana production.

The main concept of this deck is to cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn using the card Show and Tell combined with Omniscience. Atraxa, Grand Unifier helps to control the board while ramping up mana with cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb. The deck also uses counter spells like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect the combo. The inclusion of cards like Cunning Wish and Stock Up allows for additional flexibility and cards like Brainstorm and Ponder help with card selection and consistency.

This deck focuses on quickly developing a strong board presence by playing efficient threats like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler. It utilizes disruptive spells such as Daze and Force of Will to protect its threats and control the board. Mishra's Bauble provides card advantage and helps set up future draws. Additionally, the deck includes burn spells like Lightning Bolt for reach and removal. Overall, the key strategy is to apply pressure while disrupting opponents' game plans, ultimately leading to victory through tempo advantage.
